params.name: Euphronios Krater
params.description: This is the only intact krater out of the 27 painted by Euphronios. It depicts a scene from the Trojan War: the hero Sarpedon lies on the ground as the gods of Sleep and Death carry away his body, while his father Zeus watches from the heavens with sorrow. It is a work filled with both tragedy and beauty, capturing the allure of ancient Greek culture.
